routes.name: Grand Traverse: Bishkek → Karakol
routes.subtitle: Capital to the far shore
routes.blurb: The headline trip — a west-to-east traverse from the capital to Karakol. You drop out of the Chui plain through the dramatic Boom Gorge to Lake Issyk-Köl, then work along the south shore, climbing into a different gorge almost every day (Tong, Tosor, Barskoon, Jeti-Ögüz, the Karakol valley). Every single camp is below 2000 m while the daily climbs reach 2500-3500 m.

days.name: Bishkek → Tokmok
days.desc: Spin out of the capital across the flat Chui farmland.

days.name: Tokmok → Burana → Kemin
days.desc: Visit the 11th-century Burana Tower, then roll east to Kemin.

days.name: Chong-Kemin valley
days.desc: Turn up the green Chong-Kemin valley between two snowy ranges.

days.name: Boom Gorge → Balykchy
days.desc: Descend the red walls of the Boom Gorge to the lakeshore.

days.name: Balykchy → Bokonbayevo
days.desc: Quiet south-shore road past wide gravel beaches.

days.name: Tong valley + → Kaji-Say
days.desc: Climb the red Tong valley, then drop to lakeside Kaji-Say.

days.name: Skazka & Tosor gorge
days.desc: Fairytale Canyon, then a long climb up the Tosor gorge.

days.name: Barskoon waterfalls
days.desc: Big switchback climb to the Barskoon falls (below the mine), then on to Kyzyl-Suu.

days.name: Jeti-Ögüz Valley of Flowers
days.desc: Red sandstone cliffs and alpine meadows under the Broken Heart rock.

days.name: Karakol valley finish
days.desc: A final climb up the Karakol valley to the ski base, then into town.

routes.name: Issyk-Köl Full Loop
routes.subtitle: All the way around the lake
routes.blurb: A complete circumnavigation of the world's second-largest alpine lake. The south shore delivers the wild gorges and gravel climbs; the north shore is gentler, with beaches, petroglyphs and spruce forests. You sleep beside the water every night (~1600-1800 m) and return to your start at Balykchy.

days.name: Balykchy → Bokonbayevo
days.desc: Roll along the south shore to friendly Bokonbayevo.

days.name: Tong valley + → Kaji-Say
days.desc: Climb the Tong valley among red hills, then to Kaji-Say.

days.name: Skazka & Tosor gorge
days.desc: Fairytale Canyon and a climb up the Tosor gorge to Tamga.

days.name: Barskoon waterfalls → Kyzyl-Suu
days.desc: The classic Barskoon gorge climb, then on to Kyzyl-Suu.

days.name: Jeti-Ögüz valley
days.desc: Broken Heart rock and the Valley of Flowers.

days.name: Karakol valley
days.desc: Climb the Karakol valley to the ski base, then into town.

days.name: Altyn-Arashan springs
days.desc: Easier day up toward the Altyn-Arashan hot springs and back.

days.name: Karakol → Ananyevo
days.desc: Cross to the north shore at Tüp and ride west above the lake.

days.name: Ananyevo → Cholpon-Ata
days.desc: Beaches and the open-air petroglyph museum at Cholpon-Ata.

days.name: Cholpon-Ata → Balykchy
days.desc: Final north-shore cruise back to the start.

routes.name: Eastern Wild: Karakol & Jyrgalan
routes.subtitle: Karakol base + Jyrgalan gravel
routes.blurb: Based out of the adventure hub of Karakol, this loop samples the best of the wild east: the red cliffs of Jeti-Ögüz, the Karakol valley, the Altyn-Arashan hot springs, the ancient San-Tash stone mound, and the emerging gravel paradise of Jyrgalan. Camps are at Karakol, Tüp and Jyrgalan (the only nights above 2000 m).

days.name: Jeti-Ögüz valley
days.desc: Warm-up loop to the Valley of Flowers and back to Karakol.

days.name: Karakol valley
days.desc: Climb the Karakol valley to the ski base, then spin back to town.

days.name: Altyn-Arashan + → Tüp
days.desc: Up toward the Altyn-Arashan springs, then north to Tüp.

days.name: San-Tash loop
days.desc: Ride east to the legendary San-Tash stone mound and back.

days.name: Tüp → Jyrgalan
days.desc: Quiet gravel climb up the valley to the old mining village.

days.name: Jyrgalan high meadows
days.desc: Explore the jailoos and trails above Jyrgalan, then back to the guesthouses.

days.name: Jyrgalan → Tüp
days.desc: Long descent back down the valley to the lakeshore at Tüp.

days.name: Tüp → Ak-Suu → Karakol
days.desc: Back south with a stop at the Ak-Suu hot springs.

days.name: Kyzyl-Suu & Juuku mouth
days.desc: West to Kyzyl-Suu and the mouth of the Juuku gorge, then back.

days.name: Karakol → Pristan loop
days.desc: Gentle final spin to the lake at Pristan-Przhevalsk and back to town.

routes.name: Kochkor & Naryn Highlands
routes.subtitle: Interior highlands out-and-back
routes.blurb: The interior option. From the lake at Balykchy you climb to the Kochkor basin, cross the Dolon pass (~3030 m, by day, well below the 3500 m limit) to the old Silk-Road town of Naryn, and explore the wide At-Bashy valley. Big-sky highland riding, but the camps stay in the valley towns (1800-2100 m) and Song-Köl (3016 m) is admired from below rather than slept on.

days.name: Balykchy → Kochkor
days.desc: Climb away from the lake over rolling steppe to the Kochkor basin.

days.name: Kochkor → Sary-Bulak
days.desc: South up the valley toward the Dolon pass approaches.

days.name: Dolon pass → Naryn
days.desc: Over the Dolon pass (~3030 m) and down to Naryn.

days.name: Naryn → At-Bashy
days.desc: Down the Naryn river then up into the broad At-Bashy valley.

days.name: At-Bashy valley loop
days.desc: Ride deep into the wide valley toward the western foothills, then back.

days.name: At-Bashy → Naryn
days.desc: Return to Naryn with time for the bazaar and a hot meal.

days.name: Salkyn-Tör forest
days.desc: Spin up to the spruce forest of Salkyn-Tör national park, then explore the Naryn river bank.

days.name: Naryn → Dolon → Sary-Bulak
days.desc: Back over the Dolon pass, camping low at Sary-Bulak.

days.name: Sary-Bulak → Orto-Tokoy
days.desc: Down to the Orto-Tokoy reservoir, then back to Kochkor.

days.name: Kochkor → Balykchy
days.desc: Long descent off the highlands back to the lakeshore.

routes.name: Chong-Kemin & North Shore
routes.subtitle: Bishkek → Cholpon-Ata via the green valley
routes.blurb: A gentler, greener traverse for those who want big scenery without the relentless climbing. Escape Bishkek into the lush Chong-Kemin valley, drop through the Boom Gorge to the lake, and follow the north shore of Issyk-Köl — beaches, petroglyphs and spruce-lined gorges — all the way to Karakol. Low camps throughout.

days.name: Bishkek → Tokmok
days.desc: Leave the capital on quiet back roads across the Chui plain.

days.name: Tokmok → Burana → Kemin
days.desc: The Burana Tower minaret, then east to the town of Kemin.

days.name: Chong-Kemin valley
days.desc: Turn up the beautiful Chong-Kemin valley toward the alpine lakes.

days.name: Boom Gorge → Balykchy
days.desc: Descend the Boom Gorge to the western tip of the lake.

days.name: Balykchy → Cholpon-Ata
days.desc: First taste of the lake along the north-shore beaches.

days.name: Cholpon-Ata rest & petroglyphs
days.desc: Easy day: the open-air petroglyph museum and a shoreline spin.

days.name: Cholpon-Ata → Ananyevo
days.desc: Ride east past Grigorievka and Semenovka gorge mouths.

days.name: Ananyevo → Tüp
days.desc: Continue along the quiet eastern north shore to Tüp.

days.name: Tüp → Karakol → Jeti-Ögüz
days.desc: Round the east end of the lake to the red cliffs of Jeti-Ögüz.

days.name: Jeti-Ögüz Valley of Flowers
days.desc: A final climb into the Valley of Flowers, then to Karakol.

routes.name: Kegety Pass Traverse
routes.subtitle: Issyk-Köl → Song-Köl → Bishkek
routes.blurb: The big linear one, and a proper adventure. Leave the lake at Balykchy, climb the 32-Parrots switchbacks of the Kalmak-Ashuu pass to the vast high lake of Song-Köl, then work north-west and cross the mighty Kegety Pass (~3780 m) to drop straight into the Chui valley and finish in Bishkek — perfect if you're flying home from there. Mostly gravel and old Soviet mountain tracks. NOTE: two nights are spent high at Song-Köl (~3000 m); everywhere else is low.

days.name: Balykchy → Kochkor
days.desc: Leave the lake and climb south over open steppe to Kochkor.

days.name: Kochkor → Kalmak-Ashuu foot
days.desc: Quiet back-valley gravel to the foot of the 32-Parrots climb.

days.name: Kalmak-Ashuu → Song-Köl
days.desc: Switchback up the pass onto the huge alpine plateau of Song-Köl.

days.name: Song-Köl shore
days.desc: Ride the wild lakeshore track past summer yurt camps.

days.name: Song-Köl → Kochkor
days.desc: Descend the 32-Parrots to Sary-Bulak and roll down to Kochkor to resupply.

days.name: Kochkor → Kegety foot
days.desc: Turn up the remote Karakol valley toward the foot of the Kegety pass.

days.name: Kegety Pass → Kegety village
days.desc: Haul over the Kegety Pass (~3780 m), then a huge descent into Chui.

days.name: Kegety → Issyk-Ata → Alamedin
days.desc: Roll along the foothills past the Issyk-Ata hot springs.

days.name: Alamedin → Bishkek
days.desc: Drop down into Bishkek and the flight home.

routes.name: Kök-Ayryk Wild Traverse
routes.subtitle: Karakol → Bishkek (no Boom Gorge)
routes.blurb: A traverse from Karakol back to Bishkek that deliberately avoids the busy Tokmok–Balykchy (Boom Gorge) highway. Instead you climb out of the Issyk-Köl basin over the rugged Kök-Ayryk Pass (~3350 m) into the hidden Chong-Kemin valley — one of the most beautiful in the country — and follow it down to the Chui plain and Bishkek. A hike-a-bike pass and quiet gravel throughout.

days.name: Karakol → Ananyevo
days.desc: Cross to the north shore and ride west above the lake.

days.name: Semenovka gorge → Grigorievka
days.desc: Dip into the spruce-lined Semenovka gorge before the Grigorievka mouth.

days.name: Chong-Ak-Suu → Kök-Ayryk foot
days.desc: Climb the spruce-lined gorge to a high camp below the pass.

days.name: Kök-Ayryk Pass → Chong-Kemin
days.desc: Over the pass (~3350 m) and down into the upper Chong-Kemin valley.

days.name: Chong-Kemin valley
days.desc: Follow the river down the long, green Chong-Kemin valley.

days.name: Chong-Kemin → Kemin
days.desc: Out of the valley mouth to the town of Kemin on the Chui side.

days.name: Kemin → Burana → Tokmok
days.desc: Quiet Chui back-roads and the Burana Tower (never the Boom Gorge highway).

days.name: Tokmok → Issyk-Ata
days.desc: Burana Tower, then south to the Issyk-Ata hot-spring gorge.

days.name: Issyk-Ata → Alamedin gorge
days.desc: Link the foothill gorges along the edge of the mountains.

days.name: Alamedin → Bishkek
days.desc: Final roll down into the capital.

routes.name: Song-Köl High Loop
routes.subtitle: Kochkor gravel loop over three passes
routes.blurb: A compact, almost entirely gravel loop from Kochkor built around the jewel of the central Tien Shan — the high lake of Song-Köl. You climb in over the Kalmak-Ashuu pass, ride the full lakeshore, drop out over the Kyzart pass into the Jumgal valley, and loop back through the Ötük backcountry. Short mountain days at altitude. NOTE: two high nights at Song-Köl (~3000 m).

days.name: Kochkor → Kalmak-Ashuu foot
days.desc: Warm-up on quiet gravel to the foot of the pass.

days.name: Kalmak-Ashuu → Song-Köl
days.desc: The 32-Parrots switchbacks onto the Song-Köl plateau.

days.name: Song-Köl south & west shore
days.desc: A slow lap of the lakeshore, past herders' yurts and grazing horses.

days.name: Song-Köl → Kochkor
days.desc: Descend the 32-Parrots to Sary-Bulak, then roll to Kochkor.

days.name: Kochkor → Köl-Ükök trail
days.desc: Gravel spur toward the alpine lake of Köl-Ükök, camp low at Isakeev.

days.name: Orto-Tokoy reservoir
days.desc: Loop out to the turquoise Orto-Tokoy reservoir and back to Kochkor.

routes.name: Shamsy–Kegety Double Pass
routes.subtitle: Bishkek loop over two big passes
routes.blurb: A punchy loop straight out of the capital that strings together two of the Kyrgyz Ala-Too's classic passes. Head east along the foothills, climb the Shamsy gorge over the Shamsy Pass (~3560 m) onto the high Kilemche plateau, then return north over the Kegety Pass (~3780 m) and down to Bishkek. Big, remote and almost all gravel. NOTE: two high plateau camps (~2800-2900 m).

days.name: Bishkek → Tokmok
days.desc: Roll east across the Chui plain, with a stop at the Burana Tower.

days.name: Tokmok → Shamsy gorge
days.desc: South to Shamsy village and up into the mouth of the gorge.

days.name: Shamsy Pass → plateau
days.desc: Climb over the Shamsy Pass (~3560 m) onto the high summer pastures.

days.name: Plateau → Kegety foot
days.desc: Traverse the wild plateau west to the foot of the Kegety pass.

days.name: Kegety Pass → village
days.desc: Over the Kegety Pass (~3780 m) and the long drop back to Chui.

days.name: Kegety → Issyk-Ata
days.desc: Foothill roll to the Issyk-Ata hot springs to soak tired legs.

days.name: Issyk-Ata → Bishkek
days.desc: Down through the Alamedin gorge mouth back to the capital.

routes.name: Naryn Backcountry
routes.subtitle: Kochkor → Naryn → Eki-Naryn
routes.blurb: A quieter, gravel-first version of the interior for those who want remote canyons over lakeshore cafés. From Kochkor you cross the Dolon pass (~3030 m) to the Silk-Road town of Naryn, then push out to the little-visited Eki-Naryn canyon where two rivers meet, and explore the broad At-Bashy valley. Camps stay in the valley towns (1800-2100 m).

days.name: Kochkor → Dolon foot
days.desc: South up the valley to the foot of the Dolon pass.

days.name: Dolon Pass → Naryn
days.desc: Over the Dolon pass (~3030 m) and down to Naryn.

days.name: Naryn → Eki-Naryn
days.desc: Follow the Naryn river gorge northeast into the remote Eki-Naryn canyon.

days.name: Eki-Naryn explore
days.desc: Ride deeper into the confluence country, then back to camp.

days.name: Eki-Naryn → Naryn
days.desc: Retrace the river gorge back to Naryn for a resupply.

days.name: Naryn → At-Bashy
days.desc: Down the river then up into the huge At-Bashy valley.

days.name: At-Bashy valley
days.desc: Explore the wide valley west toward the foothills, then back.

days.name: At-Bashy → Naryn
days.desc: Back to Naryn along the river with the wind at your back.

days.name: Naryn → Dolon foot
days.desc: Climb back toward the Dolon pass, camping low at Sary-Bulak.

days.name: Dolon → Kochkor
days.desc: Final crossing of the Dolon pass and descent to Kochkor.

routes.name: Central Massif Traverse
routes.subtitle: Naryn → Song-Köl → Kegety → Bishkek
routes.blurb: A committing, near-straight line across the heart of the Tien Shan, ending in Bishkek. From At-Bashy you climb to Naryn, cross the Dolon pass, ride up the 32-Parrots to Song-Köl, then take the wild high plateau traverse straight to the foot of the Kegety Pass (~3780 m) and drop to the capital. EXPERT: expect up to 3 days between proper resupplies and 2-3 nights at 2500-3050 m.

days.name: At-Bashy → Naryn
days.desc: Warm-up down the At-Bashy valley and along the river to Naryn.

days.name: Naryn → Dolon → Sary-Bulak
days.desc: Over the Dolon pass (~3030 m) and down toward Sary-Bulak.

days.name: Sary-Bulak → Song-Köl
days.desc: The 32-Parrots switchbacks up onto the Song-Köl plateau.

days.name: Song-Köl → Kegety foot
days.desc: A wild, roadless-feeling plateau traverse to the foot of the Kegety pass.

days.name: Kegety Pass → village
days.desc: Over the Kegety Pass (~3780 m) and the long descent into Chui.

days.name: Kegety → Bishkek
days.desc: Straight foothill roll along the Chui edge into the capital.

routes.name: Kök-Ayryk Express
routes.subtitle: Karakol → Bishkek, the fast wild line
routes.blurb: The quickest wild way from Karakol back to Bishkek without the Boom Gorge: north shore, straight up the Chong-Ak-Suu gorge, over the Kök-Ayryk Pass (~3350 m) and down the length of the Chong-Kemin valley to the capital. Six days. EXPERT: one high camp and a long remote stretch over the pass.

days.name: Karakol → Ananyevo
days.desc: Cross to the north shore and ride west above the lake.

days.name: Ananyevo → Kök-Ayryk foot
days.desc: Through Grigorievka and up the spruce-lined gorge below the pass.

days.name: Kök-Ayryk Pass → Chong-Kemin
days.desc: Over the pass (~3350 m) into the hidden upper Chong-Kemin valley.

days.name: Chong-Kemin → Kaindy
days.desc: Ride the length of the beautiful Chong-Kemin valley to its mouth.

days.name: Kaindy → Kemin → Tokmok
days.desc: Out to the Chui plain and quiet back-roads to Tokmok (never the Boom Gorge).

days.name: Tokmok → Bishkek
days.desc: Final Chui-plain spin to the capital.

routes.name: Southern Silk Line
routes.subtitle: Balykchy → Naryn → Tash-Rabat
routes.blurb: A straight southward push from the lake deep into Silk-Road country, finishing at the stone caravanserai of Tash-Rabat. You cross the Dolon pass to Naryn, dip into the remote Eki-Naryn canyon, then ride the vast At-Bashy valley to Tash-Rabat. EXPERT: high camps at a Dolon jailoo, Eki-Naryn and Tash-Rabat (~3000 m), and long gaps between shops.

days.name: Balykchy → Kochkor
days.desc: Climb off the lake over the steppe to the Kochkor basin.

days.name: Kochkor → Dolon jailoo
days.desc: Up toward the Dolon pass to a high summer-pasture camp.

days.name: Dolon → Naryn
days.desc: Over the Dolon pass (~3030 m) and down to Naryn.

days.name: Naryn → Eki-Naryn
days.desc: Follow the river gorge into the remote Eki-Naryn confluence country.

days.name: Eki-Naryn → Naryn → At-Bashy
days.desc: Back to Naryn to resupply, then up into the huge At-Bashy valley.

days.name: At-Bashy → Tash-Rabat
days.desc: Up the valley to the 15th-century stone caravanserai of Tash-Rabat.

routes.name: Grand Traverse XL
routes.subtitle: Karakol → Bishkek, the whole country
routes.blurb: The big one: a coast-to-capital expedition line from Karakol all the way to Bishkek. Ride the wild south shore of Issyk-Köl, cut inland to Kochkor, climb to Song-Köl, take the high plateau to the Kegety Pass (~3780 m) and descend to the finish. Ten days, ~760 km, everything Kyrgyzstan has. EXPERT: a couple of nights at Song-Köl (~3050 m) and long remote stretches.

days.name: Karakol → Kyzyl-Suu
days.desc: South-shore start past the red cliffs of Jeti-Ögüz.

days.name: Kyzyl-Suu → Tamga
days.desc: Along the shore past the Barskoon gorge mouth to Tamga.

days.name: Tamga → Bokonbayevo
days.desc: Quiet shore road west past Kaji-Say and the Skazka canyons.

days.name: Bokonbayevo → Balykchy
days.desc: Finish the south shore at the western tip of the lake.

days.name: Balykchy → Kochkor
days.desc: Turn inland and climb the steppe to Kochkor.

days.name: Kochkor → Kalmak-Ashuu foot
days.desc: Back-valley gravel to the foot of the 32-Parrots climb.

days.name: Kalmak-Ashuu → Song-Köl
days.desc: Switchback onto the great high lake of Song-Köl.

days.name: Song-Köl → Kegety foot
days.desc: The wild plateau traverse to the foot of the Kegety pass.

days.name: Kegety Pass → village
days.desc: Over the Kegety Pass (~3780 m) and the huge drop into Chui.

days.name: Kegety → Bishkek
days.desc: Final straight foothill roll to the capital and the flight home.

routes.name: Song-Köl Skyline
routes.subtitle: Balykchy → Song-Köl → Naryn
routes.blurb: A clean interior traverse linking the big lake to the Silk-Road town of Naryn over the high one — Song-Köl. Climb from Balykchy to Kochkor, up the 32-Parrots to Song-Köl for a lakeside night on the roof of the country, then down to Sary-Bulak, over the Dolon pass to Naryn and on to At-Bashy. EXPERT: a night on the shore of Song-Köl (~3040 m).

days.name: Balykchy → Kochkor
days.desc: Climb off the lake to the Kochkor basin.

days.name: Kochkor → Kalmak-Ashuu foot
days.desc: Quiet gravel to the foot of the 32-Parrots.

days.name: Kalmak-Ashuu → Song-Köl
days.desc: Up onto the plateau and along the lakeshore to the yurt camps.

days.name: Song-Köl → Sary-Bulak
days.desc: Descend the 32-Parrots switchbacks off the plateau to Sary-Bulak.

days.name: Sary-Bulak → Dolon → Naryn
days.desc: Over the Dolon pass (~3030 m) and down to Naryn.

days.name: Naryn → At-Bashy
days.desc: Down the river then up into the broad At-Bashy valley.
